exemplar.title:
Investigating how the temperature change (30 Â°C, 40 Â°C, 50
Â°C, 60 Â°C and 70 Â°C) affect the solubility of 1.00 g of
Potassium Chloride (KCl) using a chloride probe when
dissolved in 100 cm3 of deionized water for 180 seconds.

detailedExemplarData.title:
Investigating how the temperature change (30 Â°C, 40 Â°C, 50
Â°C, 60 Â°C and 70 Â°C) affect the solubility of 1.00 g of
Potassium Chloride (KCl) using a chloride probe when
dissolved in 100 cm3 of deionized water for 180 seconds.

strengths: Data tables and graphs are clearly structured, labelled with units and uncertainties, and include error bars, showing clear and largely precise communication.
Uncertainties are explicitly considered and propagated into error bars, demonstrating an understanding of measurement limitations.
weaknesses: No statistical measures (e.g., standard deviation, regression analysis) are calculated to strengthen the treatment of uncertainties.
A linear fit is imposed on visibly nonlinear data without justification, and the 70 Â°C outlier is not formally tested or treated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data recording and processing is clear and precise (band 4). Consideration of uncertainties is present but with significant omissions (band 3), and data processing quality shows inconsistencies (band 3). The work overall sits in the 3â4 band but leans towards strength in presentation, warranting a 4/6.

strengths: Focused research question described within a specific chemical context (temperature range, ionic interactions, lattice/hydration enthalpy).
Thorough background section referencing literature values and linking to real-world application (medicine preparation).
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities in the methodology description (inconsistent dissolution times, lack of calibration procedure detail).
Real-world justification is brief and could be expanded for greater impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research question is clearly defined within an appropriate and specific chemical context (band 5). Methodological considerations are detailed and justified (band 4), and the procedure is reproducible despite minor ambiguities (band 3). On balance, the design demonstrates a high level of coherence and rigour, meriting a holistic score of 5/6.

strengths: Conclusion restates the observed solubility trend and discusses the 70 Â°C anomaly with reference to kinetic and energetic factors.
Findings are compared to relevant literature solubility curves and university reports, integrating scientific context.
weaknesses: The conclusion relies on a trendline that ignores the clear anomaly at 70 Â°C, reducing full consistency with the analysis.
Literature comparison is qualitative; numerical overlap and unit conversions are not deeply explored.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is supported by the data and discusses anomalies (band 3) while the scientific context comparison is relevant and integrated (band 4). The balance of partial consistency and meaningful context justifies a holistic score of 4/6.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and their impacts on accuracy are clearly explained.
Relevant and realistic improvements are proposed with explanations of how they would reduce error and enhance data quality.
weaknesses: Improvements are not prioritised based on feasibility or expected error reduction magnitude.
Outlier interpretation remains speculative without statistical validation to support the recommendation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes and explains realistic improvements (band 5) and identifies specific methodological limitations (band 4). Although prioritisation and statistical validation are lacking, the depth of analysis and relevance of suggestions support a strong holistic score of 5/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states a focused research question and embeds it in a clearly-defined context: solubility of 1.00 g KCl in 100 cmÂ³ water over a defined temperature range, with an explicit link to ionic interactions, lattice/hydration enthalpy and a brief real-world justification (medicine preparation).  The background section thoroughly explains why temperature is a relevant variable and references literature values, therefore providing a specific and appropriate chemical context rather than a generic one.  Minor weaknesses (e.g., the real-world link is only one sentence) reduce the impression of full excellence, so 5/6 is appropriate within the top band.

Processing involves averaging triplicates, calculating percentage uncertainties, constructing a calibration-free trendline and describing differences between temperatures.  However, no statistical treatment (sd, regression analysis) is performed, the point at 70 Â°C is not objectively treated as an outlier, and a dotted linear trend is imposed on visibly non-linear data.  These inaccuracies place the work in the middle of the 3-4 band.

Raw-data and processed tables are clearly structured with headings, units and uncertainties; graphs are labelled and include error bars.  A sample calculation is shown.  Minor precision issues (mis-aligned table columns, inconsistent significant figures, two different graph versions) prevent a claim of being simultaneously flawless in clarity and precision, but the communication is certainly clear and largely precise.

For every weakness listed, the student proposes a realistic and clearly-linked improvement (dual probes and recalibration, weighing boat to avoid hygroscopic loss, monitoring until plateau rather than fixed time) and explains how each step would reduce error and enhance accuracy.  The explanations are relevant and show understanding, though not fully prioritised or cost-evaluated, warranting 5/6 within the top band.

Instrument uncertainties are listed and combined into percentage uncertainty; error bars are included.  Nevertheless, standard deviation / random error from replicate trials is omitted, uncertainty propagation assumes simple addition, and the impact of uncertainty on the final trend is only superficially discussed.  This evidences consideration with significant omissions, fitting band 3-4, score 3.

The student compares findings with a textbook solubility curve and a university report, explicitly noting the similar upward trend and commenting on deviations.  The comparison is relevant and integrated, but not deeply quantitative (no numerical overlap or discussion of differing units).  This fulfils âsome relevant comparisonâ, upper end.

Three specific methodological weaknesses (probe uncertainty/calibration, hygroscopic handling on paper towel, fixed dissolution time) are identified and each is explained in terms of how it could skew the solubility resultsâsystematic error, mass loss, incomplete dissolution.  The relative impact (magnitude/prioritisation) is hinted but not deeply analysed, so band 3-4, high end, is appropriate.

Methodological considerations are described for each major variable: five temperature levels, 100 cmÂ³ solvent, fixed mass of solute, 180 s dissolution time, use of calibrated probes, stirring speed, surface area, and three repeats.  Control variables are tabulated with rationales and uncertainties quoted.  However, explanations are not fully developed (e.g., why 180 s ensures equilibrium, why only five points and three trials give âsufficientâ data).  Thus the description is solid but not exhaustive, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The step-by-step procedure, apparatus list with uncertainties and photograph allow another experimenter to repeat the work, but there are ambiguities: dissolution time alternates between 100 s and 180 s, no detail on calibration method, no specification of how temperature is held constant during the 180 s, and disposal/cleaning instructions are brief.  Because reproduction would still be possible with minor clarification, the strand sits at the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The conclusion restates the observed relationship and comments on the 70 Â°C anomaly, linking it to kinetic and energetic explanations.  While generally supported by the averaged data, it relies on a trendline that ignores the clear drop at 70 Â°C, so consistency with analysis is partial rather than full. Hence the strand fits the 3-4 band, lower end.

annotations.comment: The blank equation placeholder should be removed to streamline the uncertainty analysis.
Sample calculation equation is misformatted (â\overline{ }^3â); correct the averaging formula display.
Graph uses a linear fit on clearly curved data; use an appropriate non-linear regression or discuss why linear is justified.
The title clearly states the research question and variables, providing a strong contextual framework for the investigation.
Background text ends abruptly, indicating missing content. Review and complete the last sentence to maintain coherence.
The figure caption is unclear and contains a typo; please correct the citation and ensure the caption succinctly describes the image source and content.
Research question is clearly stated with specific variables and method, embedding it in an appropriate chemical context.
Thermodynamic background correctly calculates ÎHâââ, but clarify sign conventions and include units (kJ/mol) explicitly for each term.
Hypothesis is clear and linked to kinetic molecular theory, providing a coherent rationale.
Good inclusion of lattice and hydration enthalpies with sample equations, demonstrating understanding of energetic factors.
Entropy is mentioned qualitatively; consider elaborating on entropyâs quantitative role in solubility using ÎS values or references.
Expected graph conflicts with textual reference to a non-linear trend; align the plotted trend with the cited non-linear relationship.
Independent variable description should specify how temperature was held constant (e.g., PID control, insulation).
Dependent variable is described in mg/L but solubility is typically g/100 mL; include conversion rationale and equation.
Control variables table lists stirrer RPM but omits its uncertainty; include an estimated uncertainty for RPM.
Control table lacks justification for number of temperature points and replicates; add rationale to demonstrate data sufficiency.
Qualitative description notes exponential dissolution but lacks a time-series graph; include concentration vs time plots for clarity.
Apparatus list duplicates beaker entries; consolidate into a single entry to improve clarity.
Pre-trial uses only three of five temperatures; either include all or justify the selected subset for valid pre-testing.
Methodology omits detail on probe calibration procedure; specify solutions and steps used for accurate calibration.
Step 6 lists a 100 s dissolution time while control variables specify 180 s; reconcile these to ensure procedure consistency.
Raw-data table uses mg/L units but solubility is conventionally g/100 mL; convert or justify unit usage.
Data processing omits statistical measures (e.g., standard deviation); include these to capture random error.
Uncertainty propagation uses linear addition rather than quadrature; apply correct propagation formula $$\sigma=\sqrt{\sum_i\sigma_i^2}$$.
Comparison with literature solubility curve is relevant and well integrated, enhancing the context of findings.
The conclusion accurately reflects the data trend but should address the 70 Â°C anomaly with more critical depth.
Interpretation of the 70 Â°C anomaly is speculative; strengthen with statistical outlier tests or error analysis.
Suggested improvements are realistic but lack prioritization; discuss feasibility, resource requirements, and expected error reduction.
Evaluation identifies relevant weaknesses and improvements but should explain which weakness had the greatest impact on results.
